summary: Large fighter-system cleanup and matching PR focused on callback functions. It renamed many anonymous asm symbols and callback/data table labels to semantic fighter names, reorganized ftdata callback tables, added shared inline helper code for common item callback behavior in fighter.h, and matched or moved small repeated fighter functions across many character files. The PR body explicitly says it attempted more consistent ftdata.s naming, used inline functions for shared code across fighters, matched various small functions, and resolved merge conflicts while preserving match.
what_changed: Renamed many fighter callback functions from raw func_80xxxxxx labels to semantic names such as ftCFalcon_SpecialN_StartAction, ftCFalcon_SpecialNAir_StartAction, ftDonkey_OnKnockbackEnter, ftDonkey_OnKnockbackExit, ftFox_OnKnockbackEnter, ftFox_OnKnockbackExit, ftGameWatch_OnAbsorb, and numerous SpecialN/S/Hi/Lw StartAction functions.

Renamed per-fighter action-state data labels from lbl_803xxxxx-style symbols to as_table_<fighter> names, with examples in the diff including as_table_cfalcon, as_table_ness, as_table_clink, as_table_crazyhand, as_table_donkey, as_table_drmario, as_table_roy, as_table_falco, and as_table_fox.

Reworked asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s in a large same-size replacement hunk, consistent with the PR body's claim of more consistent ftdata.s naming and the diff showing callback table names such as ft_OnItemPickupExt.

Added shared fighter item-callback helpers in src/melee/ft/fighter.h: Fighter_OnItemPickup, Fighter_OnItemInvisible, Fighter_OnItemVisible, and Fighter_OnItemDrop. The header also kept an unused macro attempt named MACRO_ft_OnItemPickup with a comment noting that inline functions were chosen instead.

Replaced repeated per-character item callback bodies with small wrappers calling the shared inline helpers, for example ftCLink_OnItemPickup calling Fighter_OnItemPickup(fighterObj, bool, 1, 1) and many fighters forwarding visibility/drop behavior to Fighter_OnItemInvisible, Fighter_OnItemVisible, and Fighter_OnItemDrop.

Updated ftcommon.c extern callback table names from less clear names to clearer item-callback names: ft_OnItemRelease became ft_OnItemDropExt, lbl_803C1B20 became ft_OnItemDrop, and ft_OnItemCatch became ft_OnItemPickup.
Adjusted assembly references in code_800877F8.s from ft_OnItemPickup to ft_OnItemPickupExt, showing that table renames were propagated to asm users rather than only C headers.

Matched or removed small duplicate asm functions in several fighter asm files as corresponding C wrappers/helpers were introduced, with visible deletions in ftgamewatch.s and other character asm files.

Touched many fighter headers to keep prototypes aligned with renamed callback symbols, including Captain, Mario, Mars, Pikachu, Mewtwo, Ness, DrMario, Emblem/Roy, Falco, Ganon, GigaKoopa, Pichu, Fox, ZakoBoy, ZakoGirl, Link, and CLink headers.
smart_moves: The PR avoided duplicating near-identical item callback logic across dozens of fighters by extracting shared inline helper functions in fighter.h while preserving per-fighter wrapper names and small parameter differences.

Using inline helpers instead of a macro for shared callback code was explicitly documented in the diff. That keeps call sites readable and type-checked while still supporting matching-sensitive code generation.
Renaming both code symbols and data tables together reduced ambiguity between callback arrays, action-state tables, and per-fighter callback implementations.
The ftcommon.c table rename pass made callback intent clearer at central dispatch points, which helps future decompilers identify whether a table is pickup, drop, or drop-ext behavior.
The author propagated symbol/table renames through asm references, headers, variables, and C call sites, which is important in this repo because partial asm and C coexist.
Leaving fighter-specific wrappers around shared inline helpers preserved ABI/table entries and per-fighter symbol names while centralizing the behavior that actually matched.
decomp_lessons: When many fighters implement structurally identical callbacks, first identify the shared control flow and then use inline helpers plus thin per-fighter wrappers; this can reduce asm while preserving exact public callback symbol layout.
Callback table names are high-value decomp anchors. Renaming tables like ft_OnItemPickup, ft_OnItemDrop, ft_OnItemDropExt, and as_table_<fighter> makes downstream call graph work much easier.
For mixed C/asm decomp work, renames must be applied consistently in asm labels, C prototypes, extern declarations, variable declarations, and any assembly address loads using @ha/@l.
A same-size rewrite of an asm data file can still be a meaningful decomp improvement if it replaces anonymous labels with semantic symbols without changing binary layout.

Shared helper extraction should account for exceptions. The fighter.h comment says Fighter_OnItemPickup is used for all fighters except Kirby and Purin, and the diff shows Kirby/Purin retained special treatment.
Small callback functions are often good matching targets because they are repetitive and dispatch-table-driven, but they can expose naming and table-identity issues across many character modules.
Keep evidence of merge-conflict resolution and matching status in PR notes when performing wide rename/refactor work; the author stated conflicts were resolved and the branch should match.
naming_conventions: Action-state table labels use as_table_<fighter>, e.g. as_table_cfalcon, as_table_ness, as_table_clink, as_table_donkey, as_table_fox.

Special move entry callbacks use ft<Character>_Special<Move>_StartAction and air variants use Air in the suffix, e.g. ftCFalcon_SpecialN_StartAction, ftCFalcon_SpecialNAir_StartAction, ftDonkey_SpecialHiAir_StartAction, ftFox_SpecialLwAir_StartAction.
Knockback callbacks use ft<Character>_OnKnockbackEnter and ft<Character>_OnKnockbackExit.
Item callbacks use ft<Character>_OnItemPickup, ft<Character>_OnItemInvisible, ft<Character>_OnItemVisible, and ft<Character>_OnItemDrop.
Central item callback arrays use ft_OnItemPickup, ft_OnItemDrop, and ft_OnItemDropExt; code_800877F8.s also references ft_OnItemPickupExt.

The diff shows some capitalization inconsistency in Falcon names, including ftCFalcon, ftCfalcon, and ftFalcon forms. Treat those names as historical evidence from this PR, not necessarily a style to copy without checking current tree conventions.

The Mewtwo header rename in the diff changes ftMewtwoAttributes to ftMewTwoAttributes, indicating this PR also touched character-name capitalization conventions, but current naming should be verified before reuse.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted anonymous asm globals to named symbols without changing instruction bodies, e.g. func_800E2B80 to ftCFalcon_SpecialN_StartAction and func_800E2C00 to ftCFalcon_SpecialNAir_StartAction.
Renamed .data labels in asm while preserving .balign and table contents, e.g. lbl_803C72B8 to as_table_cfalcon and lbl_803C7788 to as_table_fox.
Used thin C wrappers around inline shared helpers to match repeated callback assembly patterns while maintaining per-character callback entry points used by tables.
Propagated table-label renames into direct asm address loads using @ha/@l pairs, as shown by ft_OnItemPickup becoming ft_OnItemPickupExt in code_800877F8.s.
Reduced asm by matching small callback functions in C where repeated patterns made signatures and behavior clear.
Preserved fighter-specific parameter differences in shared helpers with boolean arguments such as Fighter_OnItemPickup(fighterObj, bool, 1, 1) versus Fighter_OnItemPickup(fighterObj, bool, 0, 1).
review_feedback: No formal review comments are present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment excerpt is from the author: "ready to merge ignore the wine failures".
The PR body notes that there had been many merge conflicts, but they were resolved and the branch should merge without conflict and match.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that standardized Falcon capitalization between ftCFalcon, ftCfalcon, and ftFalcon.
Check current ftdata.s and variables.h for final names of ft_OnItemPickup, ft_OnItemPickupExt, ft_OnItemDrop, and ft_OnItemDropExt.
Search for later replacements of fighter.h inline item helpers or movement of shared fighter callback code into a C source file.
Look for PRs that further decompiled Kirby and Purin item callbacks, since this PR notes the shared helper excluded those fighters.
Audit current as_table_<fighter> naming for all fighters to see which names from PR 403 survived unchanged.
